name: PR Workflow Automation
description: Streamline pull request creation, review, and merging using GitHub CLI for faster code review cycles.
trendingReason: CLI-driven PR workflows are faster than browser-based reviews

steps.title: Create a pull request from the current branch
steps.description: Open a PR with a title and body directly from the terminal.
steps.command: gh pr create --title 'feat: add user authentication' --body 'Implements JWT-based auth with refresh tokens'
steps.tip: Use --draft to create a draft PR that is not ready for review yet.

steps.title: List open pull requests
steps.description: View all open PRs in the repository with status checks.

steps.title: Check out a PR locally for review
steps.description: Fetch and check out a pull request branch to test it locally.

steps.title: Approve and merge a PR
steps.description: Submit an approval review and merge the PR in one flow.
steps.command: gh pr review 42 --approve && gh pr merge 42 --squash --delete-branch
steps.tip: Use --squash for a clean history, --merge for a merge commit, or --rebase for a linear history.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# PR Workflow Automation
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Open Pull Requests ==="
gh pr list --state open --limit 10

echo ""
echo "=== PR Commands ==="
echo "Create:   gh pr create --title 'title' --body 'description'"
echo "Draft:    gh pr create --draft --title 'WIP: feature'"
echo "Checkout: gh pr checkout <number>"
echo "Review:   gh pr review <number> --approve"
echo "Merge:    gh pr merge <number> --squash --delete-branch"
echo "Status:   gh pr status"
echo "Diff:     gh pr diff <number>"
echo "Checks:   gh pr checks <number>"

faq.question: How do I request specific reviewers?
faq.answer: Add --reviewer user1,user2 to the gh pr create command. You can also use --assignee to assign the PR.

faq.question: Can I create a PR from a fork?
faq.answer: Yes. Push to your fork first, then use 'gh pr create --repo owner/repo' to target the upstream repository.
